info.title: av_microservice_ussd
info.description:
Release Notes

VersionDetail
4.0.161Introduced new optional parameter 'source'.
4.0.159Introduced the ability to record USSD interactions. Data gets stored in table usage_tracking.
The USSD API allows accessing the request/response queue from the USSD Gateway.
At present, Airvantage supports 10 USSD Gateway systems
  • Comviva Flares
  • Custom Digicel API
  • FlashMedia TMS3
  • MOne (TIM)
  • MOne Push (TIM)
  • Panacea
  • 6Dee
  • Truteq HDAP
  • Truteq (Custom VCL)
  • USSD/SMS Bridge

get.description: FLARES - Framework for Launching Applications enabling Rapid Evolution of Services.

parameters.name: MSISDN
parameters.description: Cellular Phone Number

parameters.name: INPUT
parameters.description: User string

parameters.name: NewRequest
parameters.description: If "1", the session will be cleared and a new session created.  This will result in the person being redirected to the landing page.

parameters.name: source
parameters.description: Can be used for branching or execution logic

get.description:
This endpoint is meant to handle a custom integration for the Digicel USSD gateway (UMM). Request includes msisdn and (optional) text
Response includes
  • title - The display text IF THIS IS A BRANCH NODE
  • message - The display text IF THIS IS A LEAF NODE
  • continue - true for continuation of current session (branch node), false as a termination indication to Digicel USSD gateway (leaf node).
  • options - JSON array of "id" and "text" attributes, representing each option the person can select. (See example response)
    NOTE #1:
    When text is not supplied, the USSD microservice will treat this request as the start of a new session. NOTE #2:
    In BRANCH nodes, where user input/options are required, the microservice returns a JSON array with said options (see example). Digicel will automatically add an additional option at the end, with the wording "0 - Home".
    All interpretation (navigation and returning to home page), is done by the Airvantage microservice. UMM simply passes the subscriber's choice that he/she entered in the phone to us.
    NOTE #3:
    Digicel UMM has a default behaviour for HOME/BACK where, upon selecting this, the UMM displays the original HOME page, without passing said session to us.

parameters.name: home
parameters.description: 1 - User chose to jump to HOME

get.description:
Huawei MOne API developed for TIM (Brazil)

Request

Header The Request Header includes the MSISDN Query Parameters The http request will include the parameter "usrInput" to represent any user responses The following setting is required in the av_microservice_ussd.setting table:
Itemvalue (example)Description
USSD_MONEPULLGATEWAY/vasportal-core/app/CreditoSpecial/SpecialCredit/av_microservice_ussd/avi_ussd_endpoint_moneThe PULL USSD gateway

parameters.name: msisdn
parameters.description: One can also pass the msisdn as a query parameter instead of it being part of the HEADER

parameters.name: source
parameters.description: Can be used for branching or execution logic

get.description:
Huawei MOne PUSH API developed for TIM (Brazil) This end-point can be used to send the landing page of another USSD menu to the subscriber's phone OR to send a simple USSD message to the subscriber. Three important properties for this end-point resides in the setting table:
Itemvalue (example)Description
USSD_MONEPUSHGATEWAYhttp://10.223.189.172:8080/The address where the Push instruction is to be sent to
USSD_MONEPULLMShttp://10.171.227.10:8080/av_microservice_ussd_push/avi_ussd_endpoint_moneULR of the local pull ussd microservice
USSD_MONEPULLGATEWAYhttp://10.223.189.187/av_microservice_ussd_push/avi_ussd_endpoint_moneOnce the push has been initiated, subsequent calls will be actioned via this reference (secondary gateway, mapped to our microservice)

parameters.name: shortCode
parameters.description: USSD Shortcode, like 677

parameters.name: pushType
parameters.description:
What kind of push message is this?
URL - pushes the landing page of the USSD_MONEPULLMS to the subscriber
TEXT - pushes the 'pushText' to the subscriber

parameters.name: pushText
parameters.description: If 'pushType=TEXT', 'pushText' is what gets sent to the subscriber.

parameters.name: source
parameters.description: Can be used for branching or execution logic

get.description:
(see https://www.panaceamobile.com/developers/ussd-integration-with-server/) Panacea Mobile’s USSD gateway also allows you to fetch plain text from a web address and display it to a user when they dial your USSD string. User responses can also be pushed back to this address and fetch new text to display, effectively giving you complete control of the campaign/service from your own server. Note:
In the case where responses originate in a 3rd-party end-point and are simply passed on by the USSD to the gateway, formatting of CR/LF is achieved in the original response by using the HTML < br > or < /br > annotation.

parameters.name: ussd_msisdn
parameters.description: This is the phone number of the user, in international format, eg: 27831234567

parameters.name: ussd_type
parameters.description:
This is the type of request. Types are:
1 = New session starting
2 = User response
3 = User closed session
4 = Session timed out

If the user closes the session or the session times out, the last active node will be called (if it is/was a response URL type).

schema.default: This is what the user input was. If it was the start of the session it would be the string (eg: *100*123#). If it was a response it will be their response.

parameters.name: source
parameters.description: Can be used for branching or execution logic

get.description:
Acts as a simple HTTP-GET proxy server. When a 3rd-party USSD controller is being used, the telco will point to this end-point, and it will, in turn act as a proxy betweem the telco and the defined destination URL. Defining 3rd-party end-point
The 3rd-Party URL gets defined in the av_microservice_ussd.SETTING table. ITEM:"HTTP_PASSTHROUGH_RECIPIENT"
VALUE:"http://......" Note: Whatever USSD Gateway is being connected to, will still require the exact same query parameters as before. This end-point is simply a pass-through.

The SMS Bridge acts like a go-between for the SMS microservice's sms_inbound table, and the USSD interface. If enabled (see below), all incoming messages into the SMS micro-service, gets forwarded to this end-point, which in turn will navigate through the USSD menu as if this was a normal USSD session. Once the response is generated that typically gets returned to the USSDC, that message will be sent to the SMS microservice's /doSendSMS IMPORTANT #1:
One needs to set up the forwarding URL in the SMS microservice to ensure all incoming SMSs are forwarded to this end-point. (See DoRegisterSMSCallback in the SMS micro-service) IMPORTANT #2:
Inform the USSD micro-service where the SMS microservice resides:

UPDATE `av_microservice_ussd`.`setting`
SET value = 'http://localhost:8080/av_microservice_sms'
WHERE item = 'USSD_SMSBRIDGE_SMSURL'

IMPORTANT #3:
The SMS Bridge is enabled/disabled by setting the
av_microservice_ussd.setting.USSD_SMSBRIDGE_ENABLED=1/0 IMPORTANT #4:
Seeing as the incoming SMS message represents user choices (mostly), and the USSD branch logic only works with numerical choices, this end-point converts any non-numerical strings into its SOUNDEX counterpart. In order to see what the interpreted SOUNDEX value for an input was, one can peruse the log4j log file with INFO granularity How does SOUNDEX work?
Step #1:
Remove diacritical marks. E.g. 'ñ' -> 'n'
Step #2:
Remove consonants. E.g 'Emergency' -> 'mrgnc'
Step #3:
Asign numeric value to each character.
full example:
Airtimé ↔ airtime ↔ rtm
r = 27;t = 29;m = 22
Airtimé ↔ 272922

parameters.name: source
parameters.description: Can be used for branching or execution logic

get.description:
The 6D API was developed for Zimbabwe (NetOne).

The Request is passed in XML to the USSDC:

Example:

In the post request, the typycal XML looks like this (with the important attributes being msisdn, and userData):

<font color=red>
<xmp>
<USSDDynMenuRequest>
   <requestId>1799581917</requestId>
   <msisdn>263713424125</msisdn>
   <timeStamp>2017/09/07 11:58:04</timeStamp>
   <starCode>299</starCode>
   <keyWord>MBCA</keyWord>
   <userData>0</userData>
</USSDDynMenuRequest>
</xmp>
</font>
(This is explained on Page 8 of the 6D API document - Attached)

The typical response:
<font color=red>
<xmp>
<USSDDynMenuResponse>
	<requestId>1800000000</requestId>
	<msisdn>9880323405</msisdn>
	<starCode>123</starCode>
	<langId>1</langId>
	<encodingScheme>0</encodingScheme>
	<transferCode>success</transferCode>
	<dataSet>
		<param>
			<id>1</id>
			<value>USSD TEXT GOES HERE</value>
			<rspFlag>1</rspFlag>
			<rspURL>http://127.0.0.1:1234/test?</rspURL>
			<appendIndex>0</appendIndex>
			<default>1</default>
		</param>
	</dataset>
	<ErrCode>1</ErrCode>
	<errURL>://127.0.0.1:1234/error</errURL>
	<timeStamp>2008/06/21 10:54:52</timeStamp>
</USSDDynMenuResponse>
</xmp>

</font>

parameters.name: reqtype
parameters.description:
TruTeq USSD enumerator
1 - New USSD request string from the mobile
2 - Response from the mobile in an already existing session
3 - Set by the Manager to indicate that the user has cancelled the current session.
4 - The session timed out. This indicates that the user has failed to respond within a pre-set timeout value.

For testing, always use '2'

get.description: Allows for the reloading of the USSD menu definitions without having to restart the application server.
This endpoint is used to retrieve the current status of the Rate Limiter as well as the status of the circuit breaker.

endpoints.description: In this microservice there is only one endpoint that is used by the USSD via the java class SubscriberForUSSD

endpointName.description: This is the class that is responsible for initiating communication with a 3rd party endpoint in the integration only one class is used SubscriberForUSSD

statusCount.description: The count of transactions the have been completed under the current status

number of slow failed calls.description: For the Circuit breaker the count of calls that are above the CB_SLOW_CALL_RATE_THRESHOLD

number of not permitted calls.description: the count of calls that have been rejected due to the circuit breaker being open

number of slow successful calls.description: the number of slow calls that have passed

number of buffered calls.description: the count of calls that are in the buffer ready to be executed

number of failed calls.description: the number of calls that have failed while trying to connect to the 3rd party

failure rate.description: the percentage of failed calls during the HALF_OPEN state

slow call rate.description: the percentage of slow calls during the HALF_OPEN state

state.description:
one of thee possible states for the circuit breaker

CLOSED: the circuit breaker is closed and calls are being sent to the 3rd party endpoint
OPEN:  the circuit breaker is open and calls are not being sent to the 3rd party endpoint
HALF_OPEN: a portion of traffic is being diverted to the 3rd party in order to determine the state of the endpoint

number of successful calls.description: The count of calls that have been successful to the 3rd party within the current state. ie OPEN,CLOSED or HALF_OPEN

number Of waiting threads.description: within the rate limiter the count of http threads that are waiting to be executed

refresh period.description: within the rate limiter the period for refresh that has been set in the database i.e 1 second etc

available Permissions.description: the number of available permissions that are still ready to be used within the above period
